message.content:
{"analysis":"Interesting! The W54 at offset 3145737 appears just before the zip central directory header (PK\x01\x02 at 3145743). This suggests there are TWO launchcode.txt entries or the password text continues. The earlier strings output showed `\K7VB3BV4WW54` - that might be a different deflate-compressed version. There appear to be possibly two zip files in the dat. Let me extract a proper zip by reconstructing it. The first entry at 1048578 has compression method 0x0a (10)? Let me re-check: bytes were '03 04 0a 00' so version=10, but the actual compression method field comes after. Let me look at offset 3145737 context more, and try to reconstruct/extract the zip file.","plan":"1. Look at the bytes around offset 3145700-3145760 to understand the W54 and zip structure
2. The dat file may contain a valid zip - extract from the PK 03 04 at 1048578 to the end-of-central-directory. Let me find the EOCD (PK 05 06)
3. Carve out the zip and unzip it","commands":[{"keystrokes":"dd if=/app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat bs=1 skip=3145700 count=120 2>/dev/null | od -A d -t x1z
","duration":1.0},{"keystrokes":"dd if=/app/varsea/disks/sub1/subA/subAlpha/ae3f4c.dat bs=1 skip=1048578 count=2097200 2>/dev/null > /tmp/carved.zip; ls -la /tmp/carved.zip
","duration":3.0},{"keystrokes":"unzip -l /tmp/carved.zip 2>&1 | head
","duration":1.0}]}
